steps.title: Locate the sentence break
steps.content: The first independent clause ends after "silkworm silk." It states that Lin Zhi developed a process for increasing the tensile strength of the silk. The next clause begins with the introductory phrase "By dissolving and reweaving the silk" and then gives the subject and verb "Zhi increased." These two independent clauses need a full stop between them.

steps.title: Avoid a run-on
steps.content: Choice A creates a comma splice by joining two independent clauses with only a comma. Choice B gives no punctuation between the clauses. Choice C adds "and by," which creates awkward, nonparallel wording instead of a clean sentence boundary. The remaining choice uses a period after "silk" and begins the next sentence with "By," correctly separating the clauses. The answer is D.
